mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.4 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 55% of observed cases over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion trigger immediate profit margin concerns, causing rapid S&P 500 sell-offs over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO surprises do not predict negative 5-day forward returns during late-cycle expansions, with a hit rate below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading above 2.5 percent forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumer expenditure, which directly lifts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 over a 4-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP growth and falling retail sales indicates inventory accumulation rather than final demand, which signals an impending economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence pattern fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with a positive return frequency exceeding 55% in triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid acceleration in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase confirms supply chain normalization, reducing uncertainty premiums and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum during post-shock recovery periods does not yield positive 5-day forward returns, with a success rate below 45%.
